hevy-ab-scissors: Lie face up with your lower back pressed to the floor and legs extended upward.
Lower one leg toward the floor without touching it while the other stays up.
Alternate legs continuously in a scissor motion, keeping your abs braced.
Control the movement and avoid arching your lower back.
hevy-bicycle-crunch: Lie face up with your hands behind your head and legs raised.
Bring one elbow toward the opposite knee while extending the other leg.
Alternate sides in a pedaling motion, rotating your torso.
Keep a controlled pace and your lower back on the floor.
hevy-bicycle-crunch-raised-legs: Lie face up with your legs raised and bent at 90 degrees.
Without lowering your legs, bring one elbow toward the opposite knee, rotating your torso.
Alternate sides keeping your legs elevated the whole time.
Brace your abs each rep and control the descent.
hevy-cable-core-palloff-press: Stand side-on to a chest-height pulley and grab the handle with both hands.
Step away to tension the cable and hold the handle at the center of your chest.
Extend your arms forward, resisting the rotation the pulley creates.
Return to your chest under control without letting your torso rotate.
hevy-cable-twist-down-to-up: Stand side-on to a low pulley and grab the handle with both hands.
With arms extended, drive the handle diagonally from your hip up and across.
Rotate your torso from the core to follow the movement.
Return to the start under control and repeat before switching sides.
hevy-cable-twist-up-to-down: Stand side-on to a high pulley and grab the handle with both hands above your shoulder.
With arms extended, drive the handle diagonally toward the opposite hip.
Rotate your torso from the core as you go down.
Return up under control and repeat before switching sides.
hevy-dragonfly: Lie on a bench and grip firmly behind your head.
Raise your body almost straight, resting on your upper back.
Lower your body slowly, keeping it extended and your abs braced.
Stop before touching the bench and rise back up with control.
hevy-hanging-knee-raise: Hang from a bar with arms extended and body stable.
Raise your knees toward your chest, contracting your abs.
Lower your legs under control to the starting position.
hevy-heel-taps: Lie face up with knees bent and feet flat.
Lift your shoulders slightly off the floor, bracing your abs.
Reach to one side and tap your heel with your hand, then the other side.
Alternate the taps keeping tension in your obliques.
hevy-hollow-rock: Lie face up and raise your legs and shoulders into a hollow (concave) position.
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor and arms extended overhead.
Rock back and forth like a rocker without losing the shape.
Control the movement from your abs at all times.
hevy-jack-knife-suspension: Place your feet in the suspension straps and hands on the floor in a plank.
Brace your abs and pull your knees toward your chest, sliding your feet in.
Keep your hips stable and your back neutral.
Extend your legs back out under control to the plank.
hevy-knee-raise-parallel-bars: Support yourself on the parallel bars, holding your body with your arms.
With a stable back, raise your knees toward your chest.
Contract your abs at the top of the movement.
Lower your legs under control without swinging.
hevy-l-sit-hold: Support yourself on two surfaces or parallel bars with arms extended.
Raise your straight legs out front, forming an 'L' with your body.
Keep your abs braced and shoulders depressed.
Hold the position for the prescribed time without dropping your legs.
hevy-landmine-180: Hold the end of a landmine bar with both hands and arms extended in front.
With feet planted, arc the bar from one side to the other overhead.
Rotate your torso and hips to follow, controlling from the core.
Alternate sides continuously and under control.
hevy-leg-raise-parallel-bars: Support yourself on the parallel bars, holding your body with your arms.
With legs extended, raise them out front to hip height or higher.
Brace your abs and avoid swinging.
Lower your legs slowly back to vertical.
hevy-lying-knee-raise: Lie face up with legs extended and hands at your sides or under your glutes.
Bend your knees and bring them toward your chest, contracting your abs.
Lift your hips slightly off the floor at the top.
Extend your legs back out under control without touching the floor.
hevy-lying-leg-raise: Lie face up with legs extended and hands under your glutes.
With straight legs, raise them until they are perpendicular to the floor.
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor.
Lower your legs under control without touching the floor.
hevy-oblique-crunch: Lie on your side with knees bent and one hand behind your head.
Raise your torso laterally, bringing your ribs toward your hip.
Contract the oblique at the top of the movement.
Lower with control and finish the reps before switching sides.
hevy-spiderman: Get into a plank with arms extended and body straight.
Bring one knee toward the elbow on the same side, outside your body.
Return to the plank and repeat with the other leg.
Keep your hips low and abs braced throughout.
hevy-toe-touch: Lie face up with your legs extended toward the ceiling.
Raise your torso, reaching your hands toward your toes.
Contract your abs at the top of the movement.
Lower your shoulders to the floor under control.
hevy-v-up: Lie face up with arms and legs extended.
Simultaneously raise your torso and straight legs into a 'V'.
Touch your feet with your hands at the highest point.
Lower arms and legs with control without touching the floor.
hevy-band-pullaparts: Stand tall holding a resistance band in front with both hands at chest height.
With arms extended, pull your hands apart, taking the band out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together as you open your arms.
Return to center under control, keeping tension on the band.
hevy-chest-supported-reverse-fly-dumbbel: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand.
With arms hanging and elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top, working the rear delts.
Lower the dumbbells under control to the start.
hevy-chest-supported-y-raise-dumbbell: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand.
Raise your arms forward and out to form a 'Y' with your body.
Keep your thumbs up and shoulder blades active.
hevy-handstand-push-up: Get into a handstand with your feet against the wall.
With your body aligned, bend your elbows lowering your head toward the floor.
Push hard to extend your arms back up.
Keep your core tight and control throughout the range.
hevy-pike-pushup: Get into an inverted 'V' with hips high and hands and feet on the floor.
Bend your elbows lowering your head toward the floor between your hands.
Push to extend your arms back up.
Keep your hips high to focus the work on your shoulders.
hevy-push-press: Stand with the bar racked on your shoulders and feet hip-width apart.
Slightly bend your knees and extend your legs with drive.
Use that momentum to press the bar overhead.
Lower the bar to your shoulders under control and repeat.
hevy-rear-delt-reverse-fly-cable: Stand in the middle of a cable crossover station holding the crossed handles.
With arms in front and elbows slightly bent, open them out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades, working the rear delts.
hevy-rear-delt-reverse-fly-dumbbell: Bend forward with a dumbbell in each hand and a straight back.
With elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top.
Lower under control to the starting position.
hevy-rear-delt-reverse-fly-machine: Sit at the pec deck facing the pad and grab the handles in front.
Open your arms back, taking the handles out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades, working the rear delts.
Return to the front under control.
hevy-reverse-fly-single-arm-cable: Stand side-on to a pulley and grab the handle with the far hand, crossing in front.
With your elbow slightly bent, take your arm back and out.
Contract the rear delt at the end of the range.
Return to the front with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-seated-overhead-press-barbell: Sit on a bench with a backrest and the bar resting on your upper chest.
Press the bar up until your arms are extended overhead.
Keep your core tight and avoid arching your back.
Lower the bar to your chest under control.
hevy-seated-overhead-press-dumbbell: Sit on a bench with a backrest and a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
Press the dumbbells up until your arms are extended.
Keep your core tight and your back supported.
Lower the dumbbells to your shoulders with control.
hevy-shoulder-press-machine-plates: Sit at the plate-loaded shoulder press machine and grab the handles.
Press the handles up until your arms are extended.
Keep your back supported and your core tight.
Lower under control to shoulder height.
hevy-shoulder-taps: Get into a plank with arms extended and body straight.
Lift one hand and tap the opposite shoulder without moving your hips.
Return the hand to the floor and alternate with the other.
Keep your hips stable and core braced throughout.
hevy-single-arm-landmine-press-barbell: Hold the end of a landmine bar with one hand at shoulder height.
Press the bar up and forward until your arm is extended.
Keep your core tight and avoid rotating your torso.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-single-arm-lateral-raise-cable: Stand side-on to a low pulley and grab the handle with the far hand.
With your elbow slightly bent, raise your arm out to shoulder height.
Contract the lateral delt at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-21s-bicep-curl: Hold the bar or dumbbells with a supinated grip and arms extended.
Do 7 reps flexing only the lower half of the range.
Do 7 reps flexing only the upper half.
Finish with 7 full reps keeping tension on the biceps.
hevy-ez-bar-biceps-curl: Stand tall holding an EZ bar with a supinated grip and arms extended.
Bend your elbows, raising the bar toward your shoulders.
Squeeze the biceps at the top without swinging your body.
Lower the bar under control until your arms are extended.
hevy-hammer-curl-band: Stand on a band holding the ends with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Bend your elbows, raising your hands toward your shoulders.
Keep your wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Lower with control, resisting the band tension.
hevy-hammer-curl-cable: Attach a rope to a low pulley and grab the ends with a neutral grip.
Bend your elbows, raising the rope toward your shoulders.
Keep your wrists neutral and elbows pinned to your torso.
Lower under control until your arms are extended.
hevy-hammer-curl-dumbbell: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand and a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Bend your elbows, raising the dumbbells toward your shoulders.
Keep your wrists neutral and elbows fixed.
Lower with control until your arms are extended.
hevy-pinwheel-curl-dumbbell: Stand with a dumbbell in a neutral grip and your arm extended.
Bend your elbow, bringing the dumbbell diagonally toward the opposite shoulder.
Squeeze the biceps at the top, keeping your elbow fixed.
Lower with control and alternate arms.
hevy-reverse-grip-concentration-curl: Sit and lean forward, resting your elbow on your inner thigh, with a pronated grip (palm down).
Bend your elbow, raising the dumbbell with your wrist facing down.
Contract your forearm and biceps at the top.
Lower under control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-rope-cable-curl: Attach a rope to a low pulley and grab the ends with a neutral grip.
Bend your elbows, bringing the rope toward your shoulders and spreading your hands at the top.
Squeeze the biceps at the top.
hevy-seated-incline-curl-dumbbell: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand and arms hanging behind your torso.
Bend your elbows, raising the dumbbells toward your shoulders.
Keep your elbows fixed to maximize the biceps stretch.
Lower with control until your arms are fully extended.
hevy-single-arm-curl-cable: Stand facing a low pulley and grab the handle with one hand, palm up.
Bend your elbow, bringing the handle toward your shoulder.
Squeeze the biceps at the top without moving your elbow.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-diamond-push-up: Get into a plank with your hands together forming a diamond under your chest.
Bend your elbows, lowering your chest toward your hands.
Push back up to extend your arms, focusing on the triceps.
Keep your body straight and core tight.
hevy-floor-triceps-dip: Sit on the floor with hands behind you and fingers pointing forward.
Lift your hips and bend your elbows, lowering your body.
Push through your triceps to extend your arms back up.
Keep your elbows pointing back throughout the movement.
hevy-seated-dip-machine: Sit at the dip machine and grab the handles at your sides.
Push the handles down, extending your elbows.
Contract your triceps at the bottom.
Return under control to the starting position.
hevy-single-arm-tricep-extension-dumbbel: Sit or stand with a dumbbell held overhead in one hand.
Bend your elbow, lowering the dumbbell behind your head.
Extend your elbow, raising the dumbbell up while keeping your elbow fixed.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-single-arm-triceps-pushdown-cable: Stand facing a high pulley and grab the handle with one hand.
With your elbow pinned to your torso, extend your arm downward.
Contract the triceps at the end of the range.
Return under control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-triceps-dip: Support yourself on two parallel bars with arms extended and body suspended.
Bend your elbows, lowering your body with your torso vertical.
Push back up to extend your arms.
Keep your elbows close to your body to focus the triceps.
hevy-triceps-dip-assisted: Get on the assisted dip machine, resting your knees on the platform.
With arms extended, bend your elbows, lowering your body.
Push back up with help from the assistance.
Keep your torso vertical and elbows close to your body.
hevy-triceps-dip-weighted: Support yourself on parallel bars with a weight belt or plate between your legs.
Bend your elbows, lowering your body under control.
Push through your triceps to extend your arms back up.
Keep your torso vertical and core tight.
hevy-triceps-kickback-cable: Bend forward facing a low pulley and grab the handle with one hand.
With your elbow raised and pinned to your torso, extend your arm back.
Contract the triceps at the end of the range.
Return with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-triceps-kickback-dumbbell: Bend forward with a straight back and a dumbbell in hand.
With your elbow raised and pinned to your torso, extend your arm back.
Contract the triceps at the end of the range.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-triceps-pressdown: Stand facing a high pulley and grab the bar with a pronated grip.
With your elbows pinned to your torso, extend your arms downward.
Contract the triceps at the end of the range.
Return under control without moving your elbows.
hevy-triceps-rope-pushdown: Stand facing a high pulley and grab the rope with a neutral grip.
With your elbows pinned to your torso, extend your arms down, spreading the rope at the bottom.
Contract the triceps at the bottom.
Return under control to the start.
hevy-behind-the-back-bicep-wrist-curl-ba: Stand with your back to a bar, holding it behind your glutes with a pronated grip.
Let the bar roll toward your fingers, flexing your wrists.
Close your wrists, raising the bar to contract your forearms.
hevy-seated-wrist-extension-barbell: Sit and rest your forearms on your thighs with palms down, holding a bar.
Let the bar drop, flexing your wrists downward.
Extend your wrists, raising the bar to contract your forearms.
hevy-assisted-pistol-squats: Stand on one leg, holding a support or TRX to assist you.
Squat down on a single leg, extending the other out front.
Descend as far as you can, keeping control and your heel down.
Push through the working leg to stand back up.
hevy-box-jump: Stand facing a box with feet hip-width apart.
Slightly bend your knees and jump, driving with your arms.
Land softly on the box with knees bent.
Step down with control and repeat.
hevy-bulgarian-split-squat: Stand with your back to a bench and rest the top of one foot on it.
Lower by bending the front knee until the thigh is parallel to the floor.
Keep your torso upright and the knee aligned with your foot.
Push through your heel to stand and finish before switching legs.
hevy-curtsy-lunge-dumbbell: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and feet hip-width apart.
Step one leg diagonally back, crossing it behind the other.
Lower by bending both knees like a curtsy.
Push back to center and alternate legs.
hevy-dumbbell-step-up: Stand facing a box or bench with a dumbbell in each hand.
Step up, planting your whole foot on the box and driving through that heel.
Rise until your leg is extended at the top.
Step down with control and alternate legs.
hevy-frog-jumps: Stand with feet slightly wider than your shoulders.
Lower into a deep squat, touching the floor with your hands.
Land softly in a squat and chain the next jump.
hevy-jumping-lunge: Get into a lunge with one leg forward and one back.
Lower by bending both knees and jump explosively.
In the air, switch the position of your legs.
Land softly in the opposite lunge and chain the next jump.
hevy-lateral-lunge: Stand tall with your feet together.
Take a wide step to one side, bending that knee.
Keep the other leg extended and your torso upright.
Push back to center and alternate sides.
hevy-lateral-squat: Stand with feet wide apart and toes forward.
Shift your weight to one side, bending that knee.
Keep the other leg extended and your back straight.
Push back to center and alternate sides.
hevy-leg-press-horizontal-machine: Sit at the horizontal leg press with your feet on the platform.
Release the safety and bend your knees, bringing the platform toward you.
Push through your heels until your legs are nearly extended without locking your knees.
Return under control to the starting position.
hevy-overhead-dumbbell-lunge: Hold a dumbbell with both hands overhead with arms extended.
Step forward and lower into a lunge, bending both knees.
Keep the dumbbell overhead and your core tight.
Push back up and alternate legs.
hevy-pause-squat-barbell: Stand with the bar on your upper back and feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Hold the bottom position for 2 to 3 seconds without losing tension.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-reverse-lunge: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Take a wide step back, lowering your rear knee toward the floor.
Keep your torso upright and the front knee aligned with your foot.
Push through the front leg to return and alternate.
hevy-reverse-lunge-barbell: Stand with the bar on your upper back and feet hip-width apart.
Step back, lowering your rear knee toward the floor.
Keep your torso upright and your core tight.
Push through the front leg to return and alternate.
hevy-reverse-lunge-dumbbell: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and feet hip-width apart.
Step back, lowering your rear knee toward the floor.
Keep your torso upright and the front knee stable.
Push through the front leg to return and alternate.
hevy-single-leg-extensions: Sit at the leg extension machine with a single leg under the pad.
Extend your knee, raising the pad until your leg is straight.
Contract the quad at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-single-leg-press-machine: Sit at the leg press and place a single foot centered on the platform.
Bend your knee, bringing the platform toward you.
Push through your heel until your leg is nearly extended without locking the knee.
Return with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-step-up: Stand facing a sturdy box or bench.
Step up, planting your whole foot on the box and driving through that heel.
Rise until your leg is extended at the top.
Step down with control and alternate legs.
hevy-sumo-squat: Stand with feet wide apart and toes turned out.
Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight and knees tracking over your toes.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-sumo-squat-barbell: Stand with feet wide and toes out, the bar on your upper back.
Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight and knees out.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-sumo-squat-dumbbell: Stand with feet wide and toes out, holding a dumbbell with both hands in front.
Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight and knees out.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-sumo-squat-kettlebell: Stand with feet wide and toes out, holding a kettlebell with both hands.
Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight and knees out.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-zercher-squat: Hold the bar in the crooks of your elbows, in front of your chest.
With feet shoulder-width apart, lower into a squat keeping your torso upright.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up.
hevy-lying-leg-curl-machine: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with the pad over your heels.
Bend your knees, bringing the pad toward your glutes.
Contract your hamstrings at the top.
Lower under control until your legs are extended.
hevy-nordic-hamstrings-curls: Kneel with your ankles secured by a partner or support.
With your body straight, lower your torso toward the floor, resisting with your hamstrings.
Descend as slowly as you can and catch yourself with your hands if needed.
Push through your hamstrings to return to the start.
hevy-single-leg-romanian-deadlift-barbel: Stand on one leg holding a bar in front with both hands.
Hinge forward, lowering the bar while raising your free leg behind you.
Keep your back straight and feel the stretch in your hamstring.
Return to standing by squeezing your glute and finish before switching legs.
hevy-single-leg-romanian-deadlift-dumbbe: Stand on one leg with a dumbbell in hand.
Hinge forward, lowering the dumbbell while raising your free leg behind you.
Keep your back straight and hips level.
Return to standing by squeezing your glute and finish before switching legs.
hevy-standing-leg-curls: Stand at the standing leg curl machine with one ankle behind the pad.
Bend your knee, bringing your heel toward your glute.
Contract the hamstring at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-straight-leg-deadlift: Stand with the bar in front and your legs nearly extended.
Hinge forward from the hips, lowering the bar down the front of your legs.
Keep your back straight and feel the hamstring stretch.
Return to standing by squeezing your glutes and hamstrings.
hevy-calf-extension-machine: Sit or stand at the calf machine with the balls of your feet on the platform.
Raise your heels, extending your ankles as high as possible.
Contract your calves at the top.
Lower your heels under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-single-leg-standing-calf-raise: Stand on one leg with the ball of your foot on a step and your heel hanging.
Raise your heel, extending your ankle as high as possible.
Contract the calf at the top.
Lower with control, feeling the stretch, and finish before switching legs.
hevy-single-leg-standing-calf-raise-barb: Stand on one leg with the bar on your upper back and the ball of your foot on a step.
Raise your heel, extending your ankle as high as possible.
Contract the calf at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-single-leg-standing-calf-raise-dumb: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell on the same side, with the ball of your foot on a step.
Raise your heel, extending your ankle as high as possible.
Contract the calf at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-single-leg-standing-calf-raise-mach: Get on the standing calf machine with a single foot on the platform.
Raise your heel, extending your ankle as high as possible.
Contract the calf at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-bird-dog: Get on all fours with hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back simultaneously.
Keep your hips level and core braced.
Return to center and alternate sides.
hevy-clamshell: Lie on your side with knees bent and legs stacked.
Keeping your feet together, open the top knee like a clam.
Contract your glute as you open without rotating your hips.
Close with control and finish before switching sides.
hevy-fire-hydrants: Get on all fours with hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keeping your knee bent, raise one leg out to the side.
Contract your glute at the top without rotating your torso.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-frog-pumps-dumbbell: Lie face up with the soles of your feet together and knees open.
Rest a dumbbell on your hips, holding it with your hands.
Raise your hips, contracting your glutes.
Lower with control without fully resting and repeat.
hevy-glute-kickback-on-floor: Get on all fours with hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Extend one leg back and up, contracting your glute.
Keep your back neutral and avoid arching your lower back.
Lower with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-lateral-band-walks: Place a resistance band around your legs, above the knees or ankles.
Get into a half squat with tension on the band.
Take lateral steps, keeping tension, without fully bringing your feet together.
Walk the prescribed number of steps to each side.
hevy-lateral-leg-raises: Lie on your side with legs extended and stacked.
Raise the top leg toward the ceiling, keeping it straight.
Contract the gluteus medius at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching sides.
hevy-standing-cable-glute-kickbacks: Stand facing a low pulley with an ankle strap attached to one ankle.
Hold the support and extend your leg back, contracting your glute.
Keep your back straight and avoid arching your lower back.
Return with control and finish before switching legs.
hevy-hip-adduction-machine: Sit at the adductor machine with your legs on the open supports.
Bring your legs together against the resistance with your inner thighs.
Contract your adductors as you close.
Open back out under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-iso-lateral-high-row-machine: Sit at the high row machine with your chest supported and grab a handle in each hand.
Pull one arm back and down, bringing your elbow toward your hip.
Contract your lat and shoulder blade.
Return with control and alternate or work both sides.
hevy-kipping-pull-up: Hang from the bar and generate a controlled swing from your shoulders and hips.
Use the kip momentum to bring your chin above the bar.
Push the bar away as you come down to restart the swing.
Chain the reps, keeping rhythm and control.
hevy-kneeling-pulldown-band: Kneel facing a band anchored high and grab the ends with both hands.
Pull the band down, bringing your elbows to your sides.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
Return up under control, resisting the tension.
hevy-lat-pulldown-band: Anchor a band high and kneel or sit holding the ends.
Pull down, bringing your hands toward your chest and elbows to your sides.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
hevy-lat-pulldown-cable: Sit at the pulldown machine and grab the bar with a wide, pronated grip.
Pull the bar toward your upper chest, driving your elbows down.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
Return up under control, extending your arms.
hevy-lat-pulldown-machine: Sit at the pulldown machine with support and grab the handles.
Pull the handles down, driving your elbows to your sides.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
hevy-lat-pulldown-close-grip-cable: Sit at the pulldown machine and attach a close grip to the pulley.
Pull the grip toward your upper chest, driving your elbows down.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
hevy-negative-pull-up: Get above the bar with your chin over it (use a jump or step to get there).
Lower your body as slowly as possible, resisting with your lats.
Control the entire descent until your arms are extended.
Get back up with help and repeat the negative phase.
hevy-reverse-grip-lat-pulldown-cable: Sit at the pulldown machine and grab the bar with a supinated grip (palms toward you) at shoulder width.
Pull the bar toward your upper chest, driving your elbows down and back.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
hevy-sternum-pull-up-gironda: Hang from the bar with a supinated or neutral grip and your body leaned back.
Pull, bringing your sternum toward the bar while arching your upper back.
Contract your lats and mid-back at the top.
Lower under control until your arms are extended.
hevy-straight-arm-lat-pulldown-cable: Stand facing a high pulley and grab the bar with arms extended in front.
Without bending your elbows, pull the bar in an arc toward your thighs.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
Return up under control, keeping your arms straight.
hevy-wide-pull-up: Hang from the bar with a grip wider than your shoulders and palms facing forward.
Pull your body up, bringing your chin over the bar.
Contract your lats at the end of the range.
Lower under control until your arms are extended.
hevy-dead-hang: Hang from a bar with arms extended and palms facing forward.
Relax your shoulders, letting your body hang without swinging.
Maintain a firm grip for the prescribed time.
Come down under control, setting your feet on the floor.
hevy-gorilla-row-kettlebell: Stand with two kettlebells on the floor between your feet, hips hinged and back straight.
Pull one kettlebell to your hip while the other stays on the floor.
Contract your back and lower with control.
Alternate arms each rep, holding the position.
hevy-iso-lateral-low-row: Sit at the low row machine with your chest supported and grab a handle in each hand.
Pull one arm back, bringing your elbow toward your hip.
Contract your mid-back and shoulder blade.
Return with control and work both sides.
hevy-iso-lateral-row-machine: Sit at the row machine with your chest supported and grab the handles.
Pull the handles back, driving your elbows to your sides.
Contract your mid-back, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Return with control to the starting position.
hevy-landmine-row: Straddle a landmine bar and grab the end with both hands or a handle.
With your back straight and hinged, pull the bar toward your chest.
Contract your mid-back at the end of the range.
Lower with control until your arms are extended.
hevy-low-row-suspension: Grab the suspension straps and lean back with your body straight and arms extended.
Pull your body toward your hands, driving your elbows to your sides.
Contract your mid-back, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower with control, extending your arms.
hevy-meadows-rows-barbell: Stand perpendicular to a landmine bar and grab the end with one hand, pronated.
With your back hinged and straight, pull the bar toward your hip.
Contract your lat and shoulder blade at the top.
Lower with control and finish before switching arms.
hevy-pendlay-row-barbell: Stand with the bar on the floor and your torso parallel to the ground, pronated grip.
Pull the bar explosively toward your lower chest.
Contract your mid-back, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower the bar to the floor each rep, keeping your back straight.
hevy-rack-pull: Set the bar on the pins of a rack at knee height.
With your back straight, grab the bar and extend your hips and knees to lift it.
Contract your back and glutes at the top.
Lower the bar to the pins under control.
hevy-scapular-pull-ups: Hang from the bar with arms extended and body relaxed.
Without bending your elbows, depress your shoulder blades, raising your body slightly.
Contract your upper back as you squeeze your shoulder blades.
Return to a passive hang under control and repeat.
hevy-seated-cable-row-bar-grip: Sit at the low row machine with your feet planted and grab a straight bar.
With your back straight, pull the bar toward your abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Contract your mid-back, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Return with control, extending your arms without rounding your back.
hevy-seated-cable-row-bar-wide-grip: Sit at the low row machine and grab a wide bar with a pronated grip.
With your back straight, pull the bar toward your upper abdomen, flaring your elbows.
Contract your upper and mid-back as you squeeze your shoulder blades.
Return with control, extending your arms.
hevy-back-extension-hyperextension: Get on the hyperextension bench with your hips supported and ankles secured.
With your hands on your chest, lower your torso, hinging at the hips.
Raise your torso until it's aligned with your legs, contracting your lower back and glutes.
Avoid over-extending your back and lower with control.
hevy-back-extension-machine: Sit at the lumbar extension machine with your back against the moving pad.
Push the pad back, extending your lower back.
Contract your lower back at the end of the range.
Return under control to the starting position.
hevy-back-extension-weighted-hyperextens: Get on the hyperextension bench holding a plate against your chest.
Lower your torso, hinging at the hips with your back straight.
Raise your torso until it's aligned with your legs, contracting your lower back and glutes.
Avoid over-extending and lower with control.
hevy-around-the-world: Lie on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
With arms nearly extended, arc the dumbbells overhead.
Continue the arc until they meet at the top, then return the same way.
Keep the movement slow and controlled.
hevy-butterfly-pec-deck: Sit at the pec deck machine with your forearms on the pads.
Bring your arms together in front, contracting your chest.
Hold a slight squeeze in the center.
Open your arms under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-cable-fly-crossovers: Stand in the middle of a high pulley station and grab a handle in each hand.
With elbows slightly bent, bring your hands together in front in an arc.
Cross your hands slightly at the end, contracting your chest.
Open your arms under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-chest-dip: Support yourself on parallel bars and lean slightly forward.
Bend your elbows, lowering your body with your torso leaned.
Push back up to extend your arms, focusing on the chest.
Keep your elbows flared and control throughout the movement.
hevy-chest-dip-assisted: Get on the assisted dip machine with your knees on the platform and lean forward.
Bend your elbows, lowering your body with your torso leaned.
Push back up with help from the assistance.
Keep the focus on your chest with elbows flared.
hevy-chest-dip-weighted: Support yourself on parallel bars with added weight and lean forward.
Bend your elbows, lowering your body with your torso leaned.
Push through your chest to extend your arms back up.
Control the descent, keeping your elbows flared.
hevy-clap-push-ups: Get into a push-up position with hands under your shoulders.
Lower your chest to the floor and push explosively to leave the ground.
Clap in the air and set your hands back down.
Land softly, bending your elbows, and chain the next rep.
hevy-decline-chest-fly-dumbbell: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in each hand above your chest.
With elbows slightly bent, open your arms out to the sides.
Feel the chest stretch at the bottom.
Bring the dumbbells together at the top in an arc, contracting your chest.
hevy-dumbbell-squeeze-press: Lie on a flat bench with two dumbbells pressed together over your chest.
Press the dumbbells against each other and push them up.
Keep the pressure between the dumbbells throughout the range.
Lower under control to your chest without separating the dumbbells.
hevy-incline-chest-fly-dumbbell: Lie on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand above your chest.
With elbows slightly bent, open your arms out to the sides.
Bring the dumbbells together at the top in an arc, contracting your chest.
hevy-iso-lateral-chest-press-machine: Sit at the chest press machine with your back supported and grab the handles.
Press the handles forward, extending your arms.
Contract your chest at the end of the range.
Return with control until you feel the stretch.
hevy-kneeling-push-up: Get into a push-up position but rest your knees on the floor.
Lower your chest toward the floor, bending your elbows.
Push back up to extend your arms.
Keep your body straight from the knees and core tight.
hevy-low-cable-fly-crossovers: Stand in the middle of a low pulley station and grab a handle in each hand.
With elbows slightly bent, bring your hands up and in in an arc.
Contract your chest as your hands meet at chest height.
Lower under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-one-arm-push-up: Get into a push-up position with feet wide apart and one hand behind your back.
Lower your chest toward the floor with the supporting hand, bending the elbow.
Push back up to extend your arm, keeping your balance.
Keep your hips stable and finish before switching arms.
hevy-plank-pushup: Push up with one arm and then the other until you're in a high plank on your hands.
Lower back down onto one forearm and then the other.
Keep your hips stable and core tight throughout the movement.
hevy-plate-press: Lie on a flat bench holding a plate with both hands over your chest.
Press the plate up, extending your arms.
Keep pressure on the plate with your palms.
Lower under control to your chest.
hevy-plate-squeeze-svend-press: Stand holding a plate with both palms pressing it at chest height.
Press the plate forward, extending your arms without stopping the squeeze.
Contract your chest as you extend your arms.
Return the plate to your chest under control.
hevy-push-up-close-grip: Get into a push-up position with hands closer than your shoulders.
Lower your chest toward the floor, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Push back up to extend your arms.
Keep your body straight and core tight.
hevy-seated-chest-flys-cable: Sit in the middle of a pulley station with a handle in each hand.
With elbows slightly bent, bring your hands together in front in an arc.
Contract your chest as your hands meet.
Open your arms under control, feeling the stretch.
hevy-aerobics: Perform a continuous sequence of rhythmic arm and leg movements.
Keep a moderate, steady intensity throughout the session.
Coordinate your breathing with the movement.
Adjust the pace to your fitness level.
hevy-boxing: Take a guard stance with your fists up and weight balanced.
Throw combinations of punches, keeping your technique and footwork.
Keep your core tight and breathe with each punch.
Sustain the intensity through the rounds or prescribed time.
hevy-climbing: Grip the holds, keeping your weight close to the wall.
Ascend by alternately moving hands and feet under control.
Push with your legs rather than just pulling with your arms.
Keep your core active to control your balance.
hevy-hiit: Choose one or several high-intensity exercises.
Work at maximum intensity during short intervals.
Rest or slow down during brief recovery intervals.
Repeat the work and rest cycles for the prescribed time.
hevy-hiking: Walk over natural or hilly terrain at a sustained pace.
Keep an upright posture and firm steps.
Regulate your breathing according to the incline.
Stay hydrated and adjust your pace to the difficulty of the route.
hevy-jump-rope: Hold the rope handles with relaxed wrists.
Turn the rope with your wrists and jump with both feet as it passes.
Keep your jumps low and your rhythm steady.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
hevy-running: Run keeping an upright posture and your gaze forward.
Land your foot naturally and keep a comfortable stride.
Coordinate your arm swing with your legs.
Regulate your pace and breathing according to the distance or time.
hevy-skiing: Keep your knees bent and your weight centered over the skis.
Control direction and speed with edging and turning.
Keep your core tight and your posture balanced.
Adjust the intensity to the slope.
hevy-snowboarding: Keep your knees bent and your weight balanced over the board.
Control direction and speed by edging your body.
Keep your core active and posture stable.
Adjust the intensity to the terrain.
hevy-spinning: Adjust the bike's seat and handlebars to your height.
Pedal at a steady rhythm with a firm posture.
Vary the resistance and cadence according to the interval.
Regulate your intensity and breathing during the session.
hevy-sprints: Take a start stance with your body leaned forward.
Run at maximum speed for a short distance.
Drive hard from your legs and coordinate your arm swing.
Recover by walking or jogging before the next sprint.
hevy-stair-machine-floors: Get on the stair climber and hold the rails lightly.
Climb steps at a steady pace, keeping an upright posture.
Avoid loading your weight on your arms.
Regulate the speed to complete the prescribed floors.
hevy-stair-machine-steps: Get on the stair climber and hold the rails lightly.
Climb steps continuously, keeping an upright posture.
Plant your whole foot on each step.
Keep a steady rhythm for the prescribed time or steps.
hevy-swimming: Enter the water and take a horizontal position.
Perform coordinated strokes with the kick of your chosen style.
Coordinate your breathing with your arm movement.
Keep a steady pace over the prescribed laps.
hevy-treadmill: Get on the treadmill and set the speed and incline.
Walk or run keeping an upright posture and your gaze forward.
Avoid holding the rails so you don't lose your form.
Regulate the intensity to your goal.
hevy-lying-neck-curls: Lie face up on a bench with your head off the edge.
Lower your head back under control.
Flex your neck, bringing your chin toward your chest.
Return slowly to the starting position.
hevy-lying-neck-curls-weighted: Lie face up on a bench with your head off the edge and a light plate on your forehead.
Lower your head back under control.
Flex your neck, bringing your chin toward your chest.
Return slowly, holding the plate with your hands.
hevy-lying-neck-extension: Lie face down on a bench with your head off the edge.
Lower your chin toward your chest under control.
Extend your neck, raising your head back.
Return slowly to the starting position.
hevy-lying-neck-extension-weighted: Lie face down on a bench with your head off the edge and a light plate on the back of your head.
Lower your chin toward your chest under control.
Extend your neck, raising your head back.
Return slowly, holding the plate with your hands.
hevy-burpee: Stand tall and drop down to place your hands on the floor.
Kick your feet back into a push-up position and do a push-up.
Bring your feet back to your hands and jump up with arms extended.
Land softly and chain the next rep.
hevy-burpee-over-the-bar: Stand next to a bar and drop down to place your hands on the floor.
Kick your feet back into a push-up position and do a push-up.
Bring your feet in and stand, jumping laterally over the bar.
Land softly on the other side and chain the next rep.
hevy-deadlift-high-pull: Stand with the bar or weight on the floor and hips hinged, back straight.
Extend your hips and knees explosively, lifting the load.
Use that momentum to pull the bar toward chin height with high elbows.
Lower the load under control and repeat.
hevy-downward-dog: Start on all fours with hands under your shoulders.
Raise your hips toward the ceiling, forming an inverted 'V'.
Extend your back and legs, pressing your heels toward the floor.
Hold the posture, breathing under control.
hevy-farmers-walk: Stand between two heavy dumbbells or weights and grab them with a straight back.
Stand up, keeping your shoulders back and core tight.
Walk with short, firm steps for the prescribed distance.
Set the loads down with control when finished.
hevy-front-lever-hold: Hang from the bar with arms extended and a pronated grip.
Raise your body until it's horizontal, parallel to the floor, with your body straight.
Keep your core and lats contracted to hold the position.
Hold the prescribed time and lower with control.
hevy-handstand-hold: Get into a handstand, resting your feet on the wall if needed.
Align your wrists, shoulders and hips with your body straight.
Contract your core and shoulders to keep your balance.
Hold the position for the prescribed time.
hevy-high-knee-skips: Stand tall and perform a skip, driving your knee up high.
Push upward each step, coordinating your arm swing.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
hevy-high-knees: Stand tall with your core active.
Run in place, driving your knees up to hip height.
Coordinate your arm swing with your leg movement.
hevy-jump-shrug: Stand with the bar or dumbbells in front of your thighs.
Slightly bend your knees and extend your hips and knees explosively.
Shrug your shoulders hard with the drive, rising onto the balls of your feet.
hevy-jumping-jack: Stand tall with feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump, spreading your legs and raising your arms overhead.
Jump again, returning to the starting position.
hevy-mountain-climber: Get into a high plank with arms extended.
Bring one knee toward your chest and extend it back.
Alternate legs quickly, as if running.
Keep your hips low and core tight.
hevy-pilates: Perform a sequence of core control and strength exercises.
Coordinate each movement with your breathing.
Keep your spine aligned and your abs active.
Execute the movements slowly and under control.
hevy-press-under: Stand with the bar or dumbbells at shoulder height.
Quickly bend your knees, dropping under the load.
Receive the load with your arms extended overhead.
Stand up by extending your legs and lower with control.
hevy-squat-row: Stand facing a low pulley holding the handle with both hands and arms extended.
Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight.
As you rise, pull the handle toward your abdomen in a row.
Extend your arms and lower into a squat again in a coordinated way.
hevy-wall-ball: Stand facing a wall holding a medicine ball at chest height.
Lower into a squat and, as you rise, throw the ball up against the wall.
Catch the ball with your arms and chain the next squat.
Keep the rhythm and control through the reps.
hevy-warm-up: Perform gentle mobility movements for the whole body.
Gradually increase intensity with dynamic mobility.
Prepare for training by activating the main muscles.
hevy-yoga: Perform a sequence of poses, holding each for several breaths.
Coordinate your movement with your breathing.
Keep alignment and balance in each pose.
Move fluidly and under control between poses.
